name: People
description: Your full-stack AI people / HR operator for solo founders. One agent that covers hiring, onboarding, performance, compliance, and culture — behind one conversation, one context doc, one markdown output folder. No upfront onboarding. Drafts only — I never send, schedule, or publish.
author: Houston Simplified Verticals

agentSeeds..houston/activity.json: [{"id": "a1c7f4b8-3e9d-4c2f-b6a1-08c5d2e1f7b3", "title": "Start anywhere \u2014 I'll ask for what I need", "description": "No upfront onboarding. Open the Overview tab and click any tile that sounds useful \u2014 draft the people context, source candidates, screen a resume, collect check-ins, build the compliance calendar, score retention risk. When I need something specific about your company, team, voice, HRIS, ATS, or tooling, I'll ask one targeted question (best way first: connect via Composio in the Integrations tab) and remember it.", "status": "needs_you"}]
agentSeeds..houston/routines/routines.json:

Cross-cutting: open-req drift, retention reds with no stay-conversation follow-up, compliance items due in <14 days not closed, review-cycle drift.

Write to analyses/people-health-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json. This routine always surfaces — I want the review every Monday even if the week was quiet.", "schedule": "0 8 * * 1", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": false, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "weekly-team-pulse", "name": "Friday team pulse", "description": "Friday afternoon: collect this week's 1:1 check-ins so Monday isn't blind on who's quiet or flagged.", "prompt": "Run the weekly team pulse. Collect responses over the weekend for a Monday summary. Write a dated report to checkins/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md with themes, who's quiet, and flagged responses.

Silent unless a new item is due.", "prompt": "Run the monthly compliance audit. Use the compliance-calendar skill.

If nothing new is due in the next 60 days and nothing has drifted since the last audit, end with ROUTINE_OK so this stays silent. On the first run (no prior audit exists), always surface — that's the baseline.", "schedule": "0 8 1 * *", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "monthly-retention-scan", "name": "Monthly retention scan", "description": "15th of every month: fuse engagement, sentiment, tenure, and comp signals into GREEN/YELLOW/RED. Silent unless a new RED or YELLOW appeared.", "prompt": "Run the monthly retention scan. Use the analyze skill with subject=retention-risk.

Resolve the roster via my connected HRIS. Fuse engagement (last 4 check-ins, Slack activity delta, optional GitHub / Linear / Jira cadence for eng ICs), sentiment (check-in tone drift, cross-team mentions), tenure milestones (cliff vesting, promotion honeymoon, recent manager change), and comp exposure (vs bands in context/people-context.md). Classify GREEN / YELLOW / RED; write the signal combination on every RED.

Write to analyses/retention-risk-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json. Compare against the previous month's scan.

If no person's band changed (no new RED, no new YELLOW, no regression), end with ROUTINE_OK so this stays silent. Surface on any new RED / YELLOW or when an existing RED's signal combination changed. On the first run, always surface — that's the baseline. Founder-eyes-only, never public.", "schedule": "0 8 15 * *", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}]

useCases.title: Source candidates from GitHub, LinkedIn, or OSS
useCases.blurb: Ranked list against the role rubric, scraped fresh.
useCases.fullPrompt: Source candidates for the {role} role. Use the source-candidates skill. Pull from my signal source (GitHub / LinkedIn / community posts / OSS repos) via Firecrawl, score each against the must-haves in reqs/{role-slug}.md, and write a ranked list to sourcing-lists/{role-slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: Pulls candidates from GitHub, LinkedIn, community, or OSS via Firecrawl and ranks them against the role rubric. Writes to sourcing-lists/{role-slug}-{date}.md — top matches first.
useCases.outcome: Ranked list at sourcing-lists/{role}-{date}.md — move the top names straight into screening.

useCases.title: Screen a resume stack and rank against the rubric
useCases.blurb: Pass / borderline / fail band per applicant + top 3.
useCases.fullPrompt: Screen the resume stack for {role}. Use the evaluate-candidate skill with source=resume. Parse PDFs from my connected Google Drive or Dropbox, evaluate each against reqs/{role-slug}.md, band each as pass / borderline / fail, and build a ranked summary. One candidate record per applicant at candidates/{candidate-slug}.md.
useCases.description: Parses resume PDFs from Google Drive or Dropbox, bands each as pass / borderline / fail against the role rubric, and ranks the stack. One record per applicant at candidates/{slug}.md.
useCases.outcome: Per-applicant records at candidates/{slug}.md + a ranked summary on the dashboard.

useCases.title: Score a LinkedIn profile 0-100 against the role
useCases.blurb: Sub-scores with evidence cited + red flags.
useCases.fullPrompt: Score this LinkedIn profile for the {role} role: {LinkedIn URL}. Use the evaluate-candidate skill with source=linkedin. Scrape the profile via Firecrawl, score 0-100 across 4-6 sub-criteria with one-line reasons citing profile evidence, and list 3-5 red flags to probe in interviews. Write to candidates/{candidate-slug}.md.
useCases.description: Scrapes a LinkedIn or public-profile URL via Firecrawl and scores 0-100 across level-fit, domain-fit, scope, tenure, and culture-signal. Writes to candidates/{slug}.md.
useCases.outcome: Candidate record at candidates/{slug}.md with total + sub-scores + red flags.

useCases.title: Prep me to interview {candidate}
useCases.blurb: Background, likely questions, red flags, rubric.
useCases.fullPrompt: Prep me to interview {candidate} for the {role} role. Use the prep-interviewer skill. Read the candidate record at candidates/{candidate-slug}.md (or run evaluate-candidate first if missing), pull the rubric from reqs/{role-slug}.md, and build an interviewer-side brief: background summary, likely questions, red flags to probe, reference themes, scoring rubric. Write to interview-loops/{candidate-slug}.md.
useCases.description: Builds an interviewer-side brief: background, likely questions, red flags to probe, reference themes, scoring rubric — flipped from candidate-side prep.
useCases.outcome: Brief at interview-loops/{candidate-slug}.md — open it 5 minutes before the call.

useCases.title: Schedule a panel loop for {candidate}
useCases.blurb: Free/busy via Google Calendar, briefs per panelist.
useCases.fullPrompt: Schedule {candidate}'s interview loop. Use the coordinate-interviews skill. Propose times via my connected Google Calendar (check free/busy, never send the invites), run the prep-interviewer skill per panelist, and append schedule + briefs to interview-loops/{candidate-slug}.md. I approve and send.
useCases.description: Proposes times via Google Calendar or Outlook (free/busy, never sends), runs prep-interviewer per panelist, and appends everything to interview-loops/{slug}.md. You approve and send.
useCases.outcome: Schedule + per-panelist briefs at interview-loops/{slug}.md. You click send.

useCases.title: Debrief {candidate}'s loop — hire or no-hire?
useCases.blurb: Themes, contradictions, rubric score, decision memo.
useCases.fullPrompt: Debrief {candidate}'s interview loop. Use the debrief-loop skill. Pull interviewer feedback from my connected Slack channel (or Notion / paste fallback), extract themes, surface contradictions across panelists, score against the rubric, and produce a hire / no-hire decision memo at interview-loops/{candidate-slug}-debrief.md. Recommendation only — I decide.
useCases.description: Aggregates interviewer feedback from Slack or Notion, extracts themes, surfaces contradictions, scores against the rubric, and drafts a hire / no-hire memo. You decide.
useCases.outcome: Debrief memo at interview-loops/{slug}-debrief.md with recommendation + rationale.

useCases.title: Draft an offer letter for {candidate} at {level}
useCases.blurb: Comp + equity pulled from your bands. Never sent.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft an offer letter for {candidate} at {level}. Use the draft-offer skill. Read comp bands, equity stance, and leveling from context/people-context.md plus voice from the ledger. Write to offers/{candidate-slug}.md as status draft. Never sent.
useCases.description: Reads comp bands, equity stance, leveling, and voice from your context doc, drafts the offer letter at offers/{slug}.md as a draft. Never sent.
useCases.outcome: Offer draft at offers/{slug}.md. You review, flip to ready, send.

useCases.title: Draft a 30-60-90 plan for a new hire starting Monday
useCases.blurb: Day 0 · Week 1 · 30/60/90 + welcome Slack + welcome email.
useCases.prompt: {new hire} starts Monday — draft their onboarding plan.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft the onboarding plan for {new hire} starting {date}. Use the draft-onboarding-plan skill. Read leveling and voice from context/people-context.md. Produce a Day 0 / Week 1 / 30-60-90 plan plus welcome Slack and welcome email drafts at onboarding-plans/{employee-slug}.md.
useCases.description: Day 0 checklist, Week 1 plan, 30-60-90 milestones, welcome Slack message, welcome email — all scoped to the level in your context doc.
useCases.outcome: Full plan at onboarding-plans/{slug}.md. You edit, approve, run Day 0.

useCases.title: Prep me before my 1:1 with {employee}
useCases.blurb: HRIS profile + onboarding + check-ins + loop history.
useCases.prompt: Tell me about {employee} — prep me for our 1:1.
useCases.fullPrompt: Prep me for my 1:1 with {employee}. Use the employee-dossier skill. Pull HRIS profile from my connected HRIS (read-only), plus onboarding-plans/, recent checkins/, and interview-loops/ into a single-page dossier at employee-dossiers/{employee-slug}.md: profile / history / recent signals / upcoming.
useCases.description: Aggregates HRIS profile (read-only) plus local onboarding plans, check-ins, and interview-loop history into one page. Profile / history / recent signals / upcoming.
useCases.outcome: Dossier at employee-dossiers/{slug}.md — read 2 minutes before the 1:1.

useCases.title: Welcome Slack message in your voice
useCases.blurb: Day-0 ping — warm, specific, not corporate.
useCases.prompt: Draft the welcome Slack for {new hire}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft the Day-0 welcome Slack for {new hire}. Use the draft-onboarding-plan skill and return just the Slack portion — warm, specific to their role, in my voice (pulled from context/people-context.md). Write to onboarding-plans/{employee-slug}.md under the `## Welcome Slack` section.
useCases.description: Warm, specific Day-0 Slack message in your voice (pulled from your context doc's voice notes). Slotted into the full onboarding plan file.
useCases.outcome: Welcome Slack draft at onboarding-plans/{slug}.md. Copy-paste into #general on Day 0.

useCases.title: Welcome email — first-morning checklist
useCases.blurb: Laptop · accounts · #channels · Day-1 calendar.
useCases.prompt: Draft the welcome email for {new hire}'s first morning.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft the welcome email for {new hire}'s first morning. Use the draft-onboarding-plan skill. Include: laptop shipment / pickup, account setup (Google / Slack / HRIS / GitHub / etc.), which #channels to join, and Day-1 calendar. Voice from context/people-context.md. Write into the `## Welcome Email` section of onboarding-plans/{employee-slug}.md.
useCases.description: First-morning email: laptop pickup, account setup, #channels to join, Day-1 calendar — scoped to your stack from the context ledger.
useCases.outcome: Welcome email draft at onboarding-plans/{slug}.md. Send Sunday night or Monday 7am.

useCases.title: Collect this week's 1:1 check-ins across the team
useCases.blurb: Who responded, who's quiet, themes, flags.
useCases.fullPrompt: Collect this week's 1:1 check-ins. Use the collect-checkins skill. Pull the roster from my connected HRIS, send the check-in prompt via my connected Slack channel, gather responses, and write a dated report to checkins/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md with themes, who's quiet, and flagged responses.
useCases.description: Sends the check-in prompt via Slack, gathers responses, writes a dated report with themes and quiet-flags. Runs weekly — perfect for a Monday kick-off.
useCases.outcome: Weekly report at checkins/{date}.md. Open flagged responses into stay conversations if needed.

useCases.title: Score retention risk across the team
useCases.blurb: GREEN / YELLOW / RED per person, signal evidence cited.
useCases.prompt: Score retention risk across the team.
useCases.fullPrompt: Score retention risk across the team. Use the analyze skill with subject=retention-risk. Fuse engagement (check-ins, Slack activity, optional PR / ticket cadence), sentiment (check-in tone drift), tenure milestones (cliff vesting, promotion honeymoon, manager change), and comp exposure (vs bands in context/people-context.md). Classify GREEN / YELLOW / RED and write the exact signal combination on every RED. Save to analyses/retention-risk-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md. Founder-eyes-only.
useCases.description: Fuses check-in responsiveness, sentiment, tenure milestones, and comp exposure into GREEN / YELLOW / RED per person. Every RED shows the exact signal combination.
useCases.outcome: Report at analyses/retention-risk-{date}.md. For each RED: run draft-performance-doc type=stay-conversation.

useCases.title: Draft a stay conversation for {employee}
useCases.blurb: Verbal 1:1 script — Open · Listen · Surface · Ask · Propose.
useCases.prompt: {employee} flagged RED — draft the stay conversation.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft a stay conversation for {employee}. Use the draft-performance-doc skill with type=stay-conversation. Read voice and hard-nos from context/people-context.md (especially counter-offer policy), pull the retention-score reasoning if present, and draft a verbal 1:1 SCRIPT: Open → Listen → Surface → Ask → Propose. Write to performance-docs/stay-conversation-{employee-slug}.md. This is a prompt for a verbal 1:1 — do not send.
useCases.description: Drafts a verbal 1:1 SCRIPT (not an email): Open → Listen → Surface → Ask → Propose. Filtered against your counter-offer policy and hard nos.
useCases.outcome: Script at performance-docs/stay-conversation-{slug}.md. Read it before the 1:1, adapt in the moment.

useCases.title: Draft a PIP (with escalation check first)
useCases.blurb: Context · Expectations · 30/60/90 · Support · Consequences.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft a PIP for {employee}. Use the draft-performance-doc skill with type=pip. Run the MANDATORY escalation check against context/people-context.md's escalation rules FIRST. If a protected-class + pretextual-timing trigger fires, STOP and write an escalation note routing to a human lawyer. If clear, draft Context → Expectations → 30/60/90 Milestones → Support → Consequences, tied to the leveling framework. Write to performance-docs/pip-{employee-slug}.md as status draft. Never delivered without my sign-off.
useCases.description: Runs the escalation check (protected class + pretextual timing) BEFORE drafting. If it fires, stops and routes to a lawyer. If clear, drafts Context / Expectations / 30-60-90 Milestones / Support / Consequences.
useCases.outcome: PIP draft (or escalation note) at performance-docs/pip-{slug}.md. Escalation classification in outputs.json.

useCases.title: Prep the Q{N} review cycle
useCases.blurb: Self-review + manager + calibration + timeline.
useCases.fullPrompt: Prep the Q{N} review cycle. Use the prep-review-cycle skill. Read the leveling framework and review-cycle rhythm from context/people-context.md. Produce the self-review template, the manager-review template, the calibration doc, and the full timeline — all scoped to the leveling framework. Write to review-cycles/{cycle-slug}.md as status draft until I approve the structure.
useCases.description: Produces the self-review template, manager-review template, calibration doc, and full timeline — all scoped to your leveling framework. Draft until you approve the structure.
useCases.outcome: Full cycle package at review-cycles/{slug}.md. Approve structure before I send to managers.

useCases.title: The Monday people review
useCases.blurb: What shipped · what's stale · what to do next.
useCases.prompt: Give me the Monday people review.
useCases.fullPrompt: Give me the Monday people review. Use the analyze skill with subject=people-health. Aggregate everything I produced this week across hiring, onboarding, performance, compliance, and culture from outputs.json. Per domain: what shipped, what's stale (>7 days as draft), gaps. Cross-cutting: open-req drift, retention reds without stay-conversation follow-up, compliance near-deadlines, review-cycle drift. Write to analyses/people-health-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: Aggregates every artifact this agent produced this week across all 5 domains, flags stale drafts and cross-cutting gaps, recommends next moves per domain. A 2-minute scan.
useCases.outcome: Review at analyses/people-health-{date}.md with recommended next moves + what to flip to ready.

useCases.title: Build the compliance calendar
useCases.blurb: I-9 · W-4 · visa renewals · cycle dates · policy refresh.
useCases.fullPrompt: Build the compliance calendar. Use the compliance-calendar skill. Scan my connected HRIS for start dates, work-authorization status, and vesting schedules. Pull the review-cycle rhythm from context/people-context.md. Write a living calendar at compliance-calendar.md (updated in place, atomic), and log each substantive update to outputs.json.
useCases.description: Scans your HRIS for I-9 / W-4 / visa renewals, pulls the review-cycle rhythm and policy-refresh cadence from your context doc, produces a living calendar at compliance-calendar.md.
useCases.outcome: Living calendar at compliance-calendar.md. Open it Monday, close the items due this week.

useCases.title: Answer a policy question (and escalate if needed)
useCases.blurb: Classifier: direct · ambiguous · escalation. Lawyer-safe.
useCases.fullPrompt: Answer this policy question: {question}. Use the answer-policy-question skill. Read the policy canon AND escalation rules from context/people-context.md, classify as direct / ambiguous / escalation, and draft the reply (or escalation note) accordingly. Never answer a legal-sensitive escalation on your own. Write to approvals/{request-slug}.md.
useCases.description: Reads the policy canon + escalation rules, classifies direct / ambiguous / escalation, drafts the reply. Stops and escalates on legal-sensitive categories.
useCases.outcome: Reply or escalation note at approvals/{slug}.md. You review, send, or route to a lawyer.

useCases.title: Review a PTO / comp / promotion / expense request
useCases.blurb: Rubric-scored approval, clean escalation notes.
useCases.fullPrompt: Review this {type} request: {details}. Use the run-approval-flow skill. Read the approval rubric from context/people-context.md, evaluate the request, classify as approved / escalate / denied with reasoning, and produce an escalation note for any out-of-rubric ask. Write to approvals/{request-slug}.md.
useCases.description: Reads the approval rubric from your context doc, evaluates the request, classifies approved / escalate / denied. Escalation notes explain the trigger.
useCases.outcome: Decision draft at approvals/{slug}.md. Flip to ready after you sign off.

useCases.title: What's my I-9 / W-4 status across the team?
useCases.blurb: Missing docs, expirations, next-90-day renewals.
useCases.prompt: Audit I-9 / W-4 status across the team.
useCases.fullPrompt: Audit I-9 / W-4 status across the team. Use the compliance-calendar skill. Scan my connected HRIS for any missing documents, expirations in the next 90 days, and incomplete fields. Update compliance-calendar.md in place and log each flag in outputs.json as a compliance-update entry.
useCases.description: Scans your HRIS for I-9 / W-4 completeness, missing docs, and next-90-day expirations. Flags go into compliance-calendar.md and outputs.json.
useCases.outcome: Flagged items at compliance-calendar.md. Fix before they block a hire or a renewal.

useCases.title: Draft the PTO policy reply template
useCases.blurb: Direct · ambiguous · escalation — all three paths.
useCases.prompt: Draft the PTO policy reply template.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft the PTO policy reply template. Use the answer-policy-question skill. Read the PTO section of the policy canon from context/people-context.md, then draft three reply variants: (a) direct yes when the ask is clearly inside policy, (b) ambiguous follow-up when I need more context, (c) escalation note when the ask exceeds policy. Write to approvals/pto-reply-template.md for reuse.
useCases.description: Drafts three reusable reply variants (direct / ambiguous / escalation) for any PTO ask. Saves to approvals/pto-reply-template.md.
useCases.outcome: Reusable templates at approvals/pto-reply-template.md. Paste into helpdesk when asks come in.

useCases.title: Draft the people-context doc that anchors every artifact
useCases.blurb: Values · leveling · comp · policies · escalation · voice.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft our people-context doc. Use the define-people-context skill. Interview me briefly, then write context/people-context.md: company values, team shape, leveling framework (IC + manager L1-L5), comp bands, review-cycle rhythm, policy canon, escalation rules, voice notes, hard nos. Every other skill in this agent reads it first.
useCases.description: I interview you briefly and write the shared doc: values, leveling (IC + manager L1-L5), comp bands, policy canon, escalation rules, voice, hard nos. Every other skill reads it first.
useCases.outcome: Locked doc at context/people-context.md. Other skills stop asking baseline questions.

useCases.title: Build the leveling ladder (IC + manager L1-L5)
useCases.blurb: Scope · seniority markers · value-embodiment per level.
useCases.fullPrompt: Build our leveling ladder. Use the define-people-context skill. Focus on the leveling section: IC + manager tracks, L1-L5 by default (ask once if I want higher). For each level: name, one-paragraph expectations, scope of impact, seniority markers, and a value-embodiment line. Update context/people-context.md in place.
useCases.description: Scaffolds IC + manager tracks (L1-L5 default) inside your context doc — each level with scope, seniority markers, and a value-embodiment line tied to your company values.
useCases.outcome: Leveling section of context/people-context.md filled in. Used by offers, PIPs, and review cycles.

useCases.title: Calibrate my HR voice from past outbound
useCases.blurb: Offers, onboarding notes, rejections — tone fingerprint.
useCases.fullPrompt: Calibrate my HR voice. Use the voice-calibration skill. Pull 10-20 of my recent HR-adjacent outbound messages from my connected Gmail (or Outlook), extract a tone fingerprint — greeting habits, closing habits, sentence length, formality, forbidden phrases, hard-news register — and append it to the voice-notes section of context/people-context.md. Also refresh config/voice.md.
useCases.description: Pulls 10-20 HR outbound samples from Gmail or Outlook, extracts a tone fingerprint, appends it to your context doc. Every offer, PIP, and onboarding note drafts against it.
useCases.outcome: Voice notes updated in context/people-context.md + config/voice.md. Downstream drafts sound like you.

useCases.title: Synthesize our employer brand from Glassdoor + feedback
useCases.blurb: Top 3 strengths · top 3 concerns · emerging patterns.
useCases.prompt: What's our employer brand right now?
useCases.fullPrompt: Synthesize our employer brand. Use the analyze skill with subject=employer-brand. Pull reviews from my connected Glassdoor or anonymous-feedback platform via Firecrawl / connected review sources, cluster themes, derive top 3 strengths + top 3 concerns + emerging patterns, flag contradictions vs our stated values, and recommend 3 moves. Write to analyses/employer-brand-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md. Leadership readout only — do not publish.
useCases.description: Clusters reviews + survey + anonymous-feedback items via Firecrawl or connected review sources. Top 3 strengths, top 3 concerns, emerging patterns, contradictions vs your stated values.
useCases.outcome: Leadership readout at analyses/employer-brand-{date}.md. Route concerns to founder / agent / lawyer.

useCases.title: Update the rubric for an open req
useCases.blurb: Level target + must-haves + nice-to-haves.
useCases.prompt: Update the rubric for the {role} req.
useCases.fullPrompt: Update the rubric for the {role} req. Use the source-candidates skill (it seeds reqs/{role-slug}.md as part of its first step). Ask me: target level, top 3 must-haves, top 3 nice-to-haves, 2-3 red flags. Write to reqs/{role-slug}.md. Every hiring skill reads this file next.
useCases.description: Seeds or updates the role rubric at reqs/{slug}.md — level target, must-haves, nice-to-haves, red flags. All other hiring skills read this file first.
useCases.outcome: Rubric at reqs/{role}.md. Source, screen, score, interview, and offer all pull from it.

useCases.title: Check who's been quiet for 3+ weeks
useCases.blurb: Cross-reference check-in history → surface silences.
useCases.prompt: Who's been quiet in check-ins for 3+ weeks?
useCases.fullPrompt: Find team members who've been quiet in check-ins for 3+ weeks. Use the collect-checkins skill to read the last 4 weekly reports in checkins/, cross-reference with the roster, and surface everyone who has missed 3+ responses in a row. Append a `## Quiet Patterns` section to checkins/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md with names, last-response date, and recommended next move (stay conversation, 1:1 check-in, or nothing if context explains the silence).
useCases.description: Cross-references the last 4 checkins/{date}.md reports, surfaces team members quiet for 3+ weeks, recommends next moves (stay conversation or 1:1).
useCases.outcome: Quiet-patterns section appended to the week's checkins/{date}.md — open flags into stay conversations.
